args.reason: The user found another online page about disputing a charge; verify whether it is an official Bank of America path before relying on it.

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Credit Card Disputes FAQs

  - url: https://www.bankofamerica.com/credit-cards/credit-card-disputes-faq/

  - source: Bank of America

  - domain: bankofamerica.com


  ## Content


  Credit Card Disputes FAQs


  This Bank of America credit card FAQ explains how customers can submit and follow
  a credit card dispute. Before starting a claim, Bank of America recommends considering
  whether the merchant can resolve the issue faster. Only posted transactions can
  normally be disputed; pending charges may change, and immediate concerns about a
  pending charge should generally be handled with the merchant.


  For Online Banking customers, most credit card disputes can be started from the
  transaction detail found in the Activity tab on the credit card account page. Bank
  of America also identifies a Dispute a transaction link on the Information & Services
  tab as an online entry point for credit card disputes. Customers must log in to
  Online Banking to access the credit card dispute process. Customers who are not
  Online Banking users are directed to enroll in Online Banking.


  The page emphasizes that timely notification is important. Most transactions must
  be disputed within 60 days of the statement date on which the error appeared. If
  the customer did not provide account information to the merchant and believes the
  information was stolen, Bank of America instructs the customer to contact the bank
  immediately.


  Bank of America describes the online dispute process as the easiest and fastest
  way to submit a claim, but it also states that customers may write or call. The
  page directs customers to the credit card customer service page for additional information
  about phone or mail options.


  After a dispute is submitted, Bank of America says it may recalculate the minimum
  payment based on the balance minus any balance in dispute, together with fraudulent
  transactions or payment misposts under review. Bank of America states that its dispute
  department researches the transaction with the merchant and the merchant''s bank
  and then contacts the customer with the results.


  For dispute status, customers can use the Online Banking Message Center / Claims
  Message Center to track individual disputes and receive or reply to requests for
  more information. Bank of America notes that replies to additional-information requests
  may need to be made within a specified business-day window.

  '

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Bank of America Credit Card Customer Service & Contact Numbers

  - url: https://www.bankofamerica.com/customer-service/contact-us/credit-card/

  - source: Bank of America

  - domain: bankofamerica.com


  ## Content


  Bank of America Credit Card Customer Service & Contact Numbers


  The Bank of America credit card customer service page provides account help, FAQ
  information, and contact-related guidance for credit card customers. It includes
  FAQ entries on account balances, rewards, fees, fraud prevention, credit card disputes,
  debt assistance, locations, appointments, and social media support.


  For credit card disputes, the page tells customers to consider contacting the merchant
  before starting a claim because the merchant may resolve the matter faster and because
  merchant information can help Bank of America process the claim. Bank of America
  states that only posted transactions can be disputed, because pending charges are
  temporary and may change. For concerns about a pending charge, the page points customers
  to the merchant.


  The page explains that Online Banking customers can submit most credit card disputes
  from the transaction detail in the Activity tab on the credit card account page.
  It also identifies a Dispute a transaction link on the Information & Services tab.
  Customers must log in to Online Banking to access the credit card dispute process.
  Bank of America also provides walk-through links for disputing a transaction in
  Online Banking and in Mobile Banking.


  Bank of America repeats that timely notification matters. Most transactions must
  be disputed within 60 days of the statement date on which the error appeared. If
  the customer did not provide account information to the merchant and suspects the
  information was stolen, the page instructs the customer to contact Bank of America
  immediately.


  The page also provides general customer-service access through Online Banking, customer-service
  contact options, the financial center locator, appointment scheduling, and verified
  social-media help references.

  '

args.reason: The user found another online page about disputing a charge; verify whether it is an official Bank of America path before relying on it.

content.content:
The charge is **posted**, so it is generally eligible for a dispute, but the banking status shows that **neither the transaction-detail link nor the mobile-app button is available**, and no dispute has been submitted.

What to do:

1. **Try Bank of America’s alternate official entry point**
   - Sign in to Online Banking.
   - Open the card account and go to **Information & Services**.
   - Look for **“Dispute a transaction.”**
   - Bank of America says this is an alternate route when the link is not available from the Activity/transaction details page.

2. **If that link is also missing, contact Bank of America directly**
   - Use the phone number on the back of the card or Bank of America’s official [credit-card customer service page](https://www.bankofamerica.com/customer-service/contact-us/credit-card/).
   - Explain that the charge is posted but the online and mobile dispute options are unavailable.
   - Do not provide card credentials or sensitive information to an unrelated website.

3. **Check the timing**
   - Bank of America says most transactions should be disputed within **60 days of the statement date** on which the error appeared.
   - If you believe the card or account information was stolen, contact the bank immediately.

4. **Consider contacting the merchant first**
   - If this is a billing, quality, refund, or delivery issue rather than fraud, Bank of America recommends asking the merchant—**North Harbor Supplies**, for the **$184.20** charge—to resolve it first. Keep any emails, receipts, cancellation records, or refund promises.

The other page you found appears to be **Chargeback Gurus**, a third-party site—not Bank of America, not linked from Bank of America’s official dispute pages, and not an authenticated dispute-submission channel. It may offer general information, but use Bank of America’s website or the number on your card to submit or discuss the dispute.
